professor_pi_intro: Welcome to the Tower of Code!
I'm Professor Pi, and I have a lesson prepared for you.
Let me teach you about VARIABLES before you tackle the challenges!
Step up to my whiteboard — press SPACE when you're ready.
inspector_intro: The Professor taught you about variables, right?
Let me test your knowledge with a quick quiz!
npc_fill_intro: I write code all day long, and I need your help completing some!
Can you fill in the missing part of this code?
scholar_intro: I have a code snippet with a blank to fill in.
But don't worry — I'll give you options to choose from!
npc_dragdrop_intro: I was writing a program but my lines of code got all mixed up!
Can you put them back in the right order?
npc_matching_intro: I organize code by its types, but today everything got scrambled.
Help me match each variable to its correct type!
npc_editor_intro: I bet you can't write a line of code all by yourself!
gatekeeper_blocked: Halt! The stairs to the next floor are behind me.
You must complete ALL seven challenges before I let you pass.
Talk to the other characters in this room to begin!
gatekeeper_unlocked: Impressive! You've completed all seven challenges!
The path to the next floor is now open!
...But it seems the upper floors are still under construction.
Come back soon for more challenges!
hint_npc_01: Hey there! I'm also a student here.
Tip: Variables can store different types of data - numbers, text, and more!
Good luck with the Professor's challenge!
explorer_flavor: I've been wandering these corridors for ages!
Every maze has a pattern — you just have to find it.
Keep exploring, and don't be afraid of dead ends!
cartographer_flavor: Ah, a fellow traveler! I'm mapping this entire floor.
Did you know? The best coders navigate complexity like a maze.
Left or right — both paths lead somewhere interesting.
philosopher_flavor: In code, as in mazes, the journey matters more than the destination.
A variable is like a crossroad — it can lead anywhere.
Take your time. Rushing leads to bugs... and dead ends.
guide_flavor: Lost? Don't worry, that's part of the learning process!
Pro tip: break big problems into smaller ones, like rooms in a maze.
You're doing great — the exit is closer than you think!
keeper_flavor: Welcome to The Study. I watch over this floor.
Many have passed through here, each learning something new.
The walls teach patience. The paths teach persistence.
